postData.contentHtml:
The rugby world watched with bated breath as the inaugural Nations Championship kicked off, and it delivered a spectacle right from the start. New Zealand's legendary All Blacks, under the new stewardship of coach Dave Rennie, faced off against a formidable French side in a match that will surely be remembered as a classic.

In a nine-try thriller, the All Blacks emerged victorious with a razor-thin 34-32 win over France. The game was a true testament to attacking rugby, with both teams showcasing their offensive prowess. For New Zealand, Cam Roigard and Will Jordan were standout performers, each crossing the try line twice to contribute significantly to their team's tally. The tight scoreline underscored the competitive nature of the contest, keeping fans on the edge of their seats until the final whistle.

This narrow victory is particularly significant as it marks the beginning of the Dave Rennie era for the All Blacks. To start his tenure with a hard-fought win against a top-tier opponent in the opening match of a new international championship bodes well for the future. The excitement surrounding the match wasn't confined to the stadium; social media platforms were abuzz with reactions. Sentiment was overwhelmingly positive, celebrating the high-quality rugby on display, though some debates naturally emerged regarding key moments and performances. This thrilling opener sets a high bar for the rest of the Nations Championship, promising more captivating rugby action to come.
postData.title: All Blacks Triumph: New Zealand Edges France in Thrilling Nations Championship Opener
postData.summary: The Dave Rennie era for the All Blacks kicked off with a bang as New Zealand secured a narrow 34-32 victory over France in a high-scoring Nations Championship opener, leaving fans exhilarated and social media buzzing.

sources.title: New Zealand open Nations Championship with slim victory over France
New Zealand edges France 34-32 to open rugby's first Nations Championship
All Blacks beat France in thrilling Nations Championship opener
Rugby-Rennie's New Zealand claim 34-32 cliffhanger over France
